text.title: Annual ↔ Quarterly OKR Stitch Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that decouple the annual bet from the quarterly KRs. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Orphan KRs accumulate; by Q3 most KRs are tagged `bet-internal` even when strategic bets are still active.
failure-mode.detector: quarterly stitch-review log shows orphan_krs_found / total_krs > 30% AND at least one annual bet has no Q3 KRs
failure-mode.repair: In the review meeting, force a rebalance: convert orphans to bet-anchored KRs OR drop the orphan OR explicitly retire the bet. The team cannot ship a quarter with > 30% orphans.

failure-mode.description: Vanity KRs: bet_id present but the KR has no measurable outcome (e.g. "Explore AIO opportunities").
failure-mode.detector: KR text matches regex on {explore, investigate, research, evaluate, consider} AND no measurable threshold or date
failure-mode.repair: Rewrite as a measurable KR with target metric and date. "Explore" is for an objective, not a KR. The PM partners with the team to draft a concrete KR.

failure-mode.description: Bet-amnesia: by Q4 the team cannot recite the annual bet without looking it up; the bet is invisible in standups.
failure-mode.detector: standup transcripts / Q4 review notes contain < 1 mention of the bet_id over a 4-week window
failure-mode.repair: Display bet_id prominently in OKR dashboards and standup-rituals. Add a "what bet is this serving" prompt to weekly check-ins.

failure-mode.description: Retire-vs-extend decision skipped; bet dropped silently between Q2 and Q3.
failure-mode.detector: cascade file's quarter_krs.Q3 is empty for an objective AND retire_decisions array does not contain the corresponding bet_id
failure-mode.repair: Block the planning meeting commit until the bet is either retired (with rationale + approver) or extended (with new KRs). Silent gap is rejected.

failure-mode.description: KR sprawl: team has 8+ active OKRs; nothing advances meaningfully.
failure-mode.detector: active OKR count per team in the quarter > 5
failure-mode.repair: Cap at 3-5. The PM negotiates with leadership to remove or defer. The cascade audit reports OKR count per team; sprawl is escalated.

failure-mode.description: Cascade file lives in a doc tool that the team does not read; stitch invisible.
failure-mode.detector: cascade file is on a wiki / docs site with no read events in the last 30 days OR not linked from the team's primary planning surface
failure-mode.repair: Move the cascade to the planning repo or wherever the team already reviews OKRs. Display the cascade in the OKR dashboard.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
Human checkpoint
section.p: Quarterly stitch reviews are owned by the PM + Head of Product. Retire / extend decisions are co-signed. Auto-generation of KRs from cascade objectives is permitted as drafting aid; auto-commit to the quarterly OKR set is FORBIDDEN — the team commits the final KRs in the planning meeting.
